mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 4.5%, it signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in real personal income and consumer prices indicate strong demand outpacing supply constraints, driving corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in greater than 55% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over 5 years.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery periods, a monthly increase in the index of industrial production reflects rapid capacity utilization that boosts short-term equity momentum over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day returns during recovery phases, with a rank IC below 0.02 over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate expanding construction activity and wealth effects that systematically lift S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST growth shows no predictive power for 20-day returns, with out-of-sample R-squared below 0.01 across multiple macro cycles.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly inflation accelerates beyond 0.4%, it forces anticipatory monetary tightening that compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of high-inflation months, or the signal loses predictive accuracy when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling unemployment rates confirms robust labor market expansion that supports consumer demand and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint labor signal fails to predict positive returns when PAYEMS increases and UNRATE decreases simultaneously, with a hit rate below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, quarterly GDP growth exceeding trend levels sustains corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns during late-cycle phases, with a t-statistic below 1.5 on the coefficient over a 10-year rolling window.
